skill.name: let-chains-advisor
skill.description: Identifies deeply nested if-let expressions and suggests let chains for cleaner control flow. Activates when users write nested conditionals with pattern matching.
skill.summary: Suggests Rust 2024 let chains for nested pattern matching and conditional control flow.
skill.author: EmilLindfors

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backtick execution and weak cryptography indicators, but review found these are false positives. The file is Markdown guidance with Rust and TOML examples, allows only Read and Grep, and contains no executable scripts, network calls, secret access, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Findings Are Markdown Fences
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown fenced Rust and TOML examples, not executable skill code. No command invocation or user-controlled shell input was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are visible Markdown code fences and Rust or TOML documentation examples. The skill metadata permits only Read and Grep, and no executable script file exists in the scanned file structure.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Has No Code Evidence
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported weak cryptographic algorithm location is the frontmatter description for a Rust let-chain refactoring skill. No hash function, cipher, certificate logic, or cryptographic API appears at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is only descriptive text about nested if-let expressions. There is no semantic evidence of cryptography, weak algorithm use, or security-sensitive processing.

content.user_title: Modernize Rust Control Flow with Let Chains
content.value_statement: Nested Rust pattern matching can make reviews slower and hide simple control flow. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code suggest Rust 2024 let-chain refactors.
actual_capabilities: Identifies nested if-let expressions with three or more levels.
Suggests Rust 2024 let-chain replacements for Option and Result checks.
Combines pattern matches with boolean guard conditions when appropriate.
Covers simple while-let loops that can use let-chain conditions.
Checks Rust edition and minimum version requirements before recommending the pattern.
Encourages review of branch behavior before changing control flow.
limitations: Requires Rust 1.88 or newer with the 2024 edition.
Does not compile, test, or automatically modify code.
Focuses on nested pattern matching, not broad Rust architecture review.
May avoid a refactor when early returns or side effects make behavior unclear.

use_cases.title: Simplify Option Handling
use_cases.description: Review nested Option unwrapping and replace simple layers with one readable let-chain condition.

use_cases.title: Modernize Review Comments
use_cases.description: Add targeted suggestions in code review when nested if-let blocks reduce readability.

use_cases.title: Prepare Rust 2024 Migration
use_cases.description: Find places where Rust 2024 let chains can make existing control flow smaller and clearer.

prompt_templates.title: Find Simple Candidates
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Rust file for nested if-let expressions. Suggest let-chain refactors only when the behavior stays clear.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain whether this nested pattern-matching block should become a Rust 2024 let chain. Include compatibility notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Learning why a change is appropriate

prompt_templates.title: Review Pull Request Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Check these Rust changes for nested Option or Result handling. Recommend let-chain alternatives with concise review comments.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Migration Path
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan this Rust module for let-chain opportunities. Rank candidates by readability gain, behavior risk, and Rust 2024 readiness.

output_examples.input: A function unwraps a user, profile, and email through three nested if-let blocks.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends one let-chain condition and notes that the refactor is suitable when each failed match returns the same fallback.

output_examples.input: A processing function checks an optional value, validates it, and then checks its size.
output_examples.output: The skill suggests combining the pattern match and boolean checks in a Rust 2024 let chain, with a compatibility reminder.

output_examples.input: A loop pulls items from an iterator, validates each item, and processes only successful results.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies a possible while-let chain and warns to confirm that loop side effects remain unchanged.

best_practices: Confirm the project uses Rust 1.88 or newer and edition 2024 before applying suggestions.
Compare fallback behavior before and after each refactor.
Prefer let chains when they reduce nesting without hiding important side effects.
anti_patterns: Do not recommend let chains for projects pinned to older Rust editions.
Do not flatten code when separate branches communicate important domain decisions.
Do not change control flow when nested blocks contain side effects that need review.

faq.question: What Rust feature does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Rust 2024 let chains for combining pattern matches and conditions.

faq.question: Does it edit files automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides review guidance and suggested refactors, but it does not write files.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When should I avoid a let-chain refactor?
faq.answer: Avoid it when the project is not on Rust 2024 or when nested branches have important separate behavior.

faq.question: Can it review Result handling?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples include nested Result checks that can be simplified when errors share the same fallback.

faq.question: Does it require external commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill metadata allows Read and Grep only, and the audit found no executable command usage.
